dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6445 IH: Fast Track Healthcare Apprenticeships Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Johnson of Texas (for herself and Mr. Kennedy of Utah) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To clarify the time period for registering health care apprenticeships under the Act of August 16, 1937 (commonly known as the National Apprenticeship Act) and require the digitization of apprenticeship agreement forms under such Act, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Fast Track Healthcare Apprenticeships Act.
The Act of August 16, 1937 (commonly known as the National Apprenticeship Act; 50 Stat. 664, chapter 663; 29 U.S.C. 50 et seq.) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating section 4 as section 6; and
by inserting after section 3 the following:

subsection.text: In administering this Act, the Secretary of Labor shall establish a national apprenticeship system that provides, with respect to any application submitted seeking to register a program in the health care field as an apprenticeship program under this Act, that—

paragraph.text: a decision shall be provided to the applicant on the application not later than 45 days after receipt of the application; and

if a decision is not made within the 45 days after receipt, a written explanation for the delay and an estimated timeline for a determination (that is not more than 90 days after the date of such written explanation) shall be provided to the applicant.
The term health care field means the following occupations:

subparagraph.text: Healthcare practitioners and technical occupations, as described in the Bureau of Labor Statistics Standard Occupational Classification System (or any corresponding occupation in any corresponding System).
Healthcare support occupations, as described in such System (or any corresponding occupation in any corresponding System).

paragraph.text: The term national apprenticeship system means the system established by the Secretary of Labor to carry out the activities authorized and directed to be carried out under section 1.

section.text: The Act of August 16, 1937 (commonly known as the National Apprenticeship Act; 50 Stat. 664, chapter 663; 29 U.S.C. 50 et seq.), as amended by section 2, is further amended by inserting after section 4 (as added by section 2) the following:

In administering this Act, the Secretary of Labor shall establish a national apprenticeship system (as such term is defined in section 4) that provides that any apprenticeship agreement form for such system (including any employer agreement form or any disability disclosure form) shall be digitized.
